templates.text: The tutor does not ask if you are ready. It asks what you know. The difference matters.
Three questions. Not a test of memory — a test of what kind of seeker you are becoming.
Chalk dust on a dark floor. Someone was taught here. Someone else failed here. The room remembers both.
The tutor's patience is not kindness. It is curiosity about how long it takes you to crack.
You answered. Correctly or not, the dungeon has updated its record of you.
Lesson plans pinned to stone. None of them written for you — and yet they all apply.
The office smells of authority and old failure. The tutor has seen better students. The tutor has also seen worse.
What the tutor gives, it gives because you earned it. What it withholds, it withholds for the same reason.
A desk, a chair, a window that shows nothing. The tutor requires no view. Knowledge is its own landscape.
